title: Akamai Guardicore Deception and Honeypot Connection Events
dataSources: Guardicore deception and network flow events via add-on, commonly `sourcetype=guardicore:network` with deception asset labels
spl:
index=microseg sourcetype="guardicore:network" earliest=-7d
| eval is_decoy=if(match(lower(coalesce(asset_labels, dst_labels, tags)),"(?i)decoy|honeypot|deception"),1,0)
| where is_decoy=1
| stats count by src_asset, dst_asset, dst_port, process_name
| where count>=1
| sort -count
description: Any connection that touches a Guardicore deception asset is high fidelity: legitimate applications should never address those addresses or processes. Monitoring these flows surfaces lateral movement and internal scanning faster than statistical baselines on production workloads alone.
value: Dramatically reduces false positives compared to generic IDS rules while validating that deception coverage stays aligned to critical segments.
implementation: Ensure deception nodes are labeled consistently in Centra and that those labels flow into Splunk via the add-on. If `asset_labels` is not extracted, map from a lookup of deception IP ranges. Integrate alerts directly with IR playbooks; treat every hit as priority triage until disproven.
visualization: Table (source asset, decoy target, port), Timeline, Link graph (Reveal-style export if available).

references.title: Splunkbase — Akamai Guardicore Add-on for Splunk

grandmaExplanation: We use network security tools to watch who accesses what, help prevent data leaks, enforce network isolation, and secure cloud and remote access.
cimSpl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic
  by All_Traffic.user, All_Traffic.action, All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest span=1h
| sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Microseg blocks from new app deployments, missing exception rules, or planned segmentation rollouts.
